title: McCarthy’s bid for speaker to continue, says Trump backs him
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans will open the second day of the new Congress much like the first — with leader Kevin McCarthy trying to become House speaker despite losing in multiple rounds of voting that threw the new GOP majority into chaos.

title: Biden heads to Kentucky to highlight cash for aging bridge
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden's visit to a notoriously dilapidated bridge connecting Ohio and Kentucky is a chance for him to showcase accomplishments and talk up the virtues of bipartisanship while rubbing shoulders with Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell.

title: EXPLAINER: What to expect on Day 2 of House speaker election
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — What was expected to be a day of triumph for House Republicans coming into the majority turned into chaos Tuesday as interparty fighting over who should lead them in their new reign ended with no speaker in the rostrum.

title: Rep.-elect Santos quiet as speaker vote delays swearing-in
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — Republican Rep.-elect George Santos of New York, who is accused of lying about large swaths of his background and accomplishments, will have to wait to add one thing with certainty to his resume: U.S.

title: McConnell celebrates milestone as Senate Dems retain power
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are again claiming the Senate majority, but much of the chamber’s focus Tuesday was on the top Republican as Mitch McConnell became the longest serving Senate leader in history.

title: Murray becomes first female president pro tempore in Senate
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Washington Sen. Patty Murray was elected to the Senate in 1992, she says, male senators treated her with some trepidation. But now she has outlasted almost all of them, becoming the first woman appointed president pro tempore — a senior member of the majority who presides over the Senate and is third in line to the presidency.

title: Biden to host Japan’s Kishida for talks on NKorea, economy
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will host Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida at the White House later this month for economic and security consultations, the U.S. administration announced Tuesday.

title: Biden renominates Garcetti, others as new Senate begins
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden still wants former Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti, who has been shadowed by a controversy over sexual harassment, to serve as his ambassador to India.He renominated Garcetti as the new congressional session began Tuesday.

title: White House gun violence program with philanthropies ends
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — It was small, as Washington celebrations go — two senior Biden administration advisers gathered with program participants near the White House on a Thursday afternoon in December to mark the end of a little-known initiative with a budget of less than $8 million.

title: Myanmar army leader touts election plan on Independence Day
shortDescription: BANGKOK (AP) — Myanmar’s ruling military leader pardoned over 7,000 prisoners, including some political detainees, and detailed plans for an election later this year during a ceremony Wednesday marking the 75th anniversary of independence from Britain.

title: Beshear to welcome Biden, give State of Commonwealth speech
shortDescription: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Gov. Andy Beshear will welcome President Joe Biden to Kentucky and join the president as he speaks in Covington before returning to Frankfort to give his State of the Commonwealth address.

title: South Korea: Talks with US on management of nukes underway
shortDescription: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea maintained that Seoul and Washington are discussing its involvement in U.S. nuclear weapons management in the face of intensifying North Korean threats, after President Joe Biden denied that the allies were discussing joint nuclear exercises.

title: McCarthy vows to stay in speaker’s race, with Trump backing
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican leader Kevin McCarthy said late Tuesday there's no scenario in which he'll drop his bid to become House speaker despite failing to win multiple rounds of voting, a historic defeat that brought the first day of the new Congress to an abrupt, messy end.

title: Democrat voted Pennsylvania speaker, foiling GOP hopes
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A Democrat who promised to govern as an independent was elected speaker of the narrowly divided Pennsylvania House of Representatives in a surprising move Tuesday on the strength of every Democrat and more than a dozen GOP votes.

title: Mississippi governor seeks 2nd term as qualifying opens
shortDescription: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — Mississippi Gov. Tate Reeves filed Tuesday to seek reelection, marking the first day candidates for state and local offices can submit qualifying papers for the 2023 election.

title: Evers pushes for legal abortions, expanded Medicaid
shortDescription: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Gov. Tony Evers outlined a largely liberal agenda in his second inaugural address Tuesday, calling for overturning an 1849 state law banning abortions, expanding Medicaid coverage and legalizing marijuana.

title: Britt sworn in as Alabama’s newest U.S. senator
shortDescription: U.S. Sen. Katie Britt was sworn into office Tuesday, becoming the youngest Republican woman to serve in the U.S. Senate.Britt, 40, took the oath of office along with other senators who were elected in November.

title: Burgum touts North Dakota’s energy sector in State of State
shortDescription: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum highlighted the successes of the state’s energy sector during his annual State of the State address on Tuesday, while also calling on lawmakers to provide tax relief and address workforce shortages that he says continue to be a top barrier to economic growth.

title: Braun fundraising points to pricey GOP Indiana governor race
shortDescription: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — A few dozen big-money donors have helped Republican U.S. Sen. Mike Braun quickly catch up in fundraising with GOP rivals in the 2024 race for Indiana governor, with his campaign saying Tuesday it has raised about $1.5 million since formally launching his bid just over a month ago.

title: Democrats take control as Minnesota Legislature convenes
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Democrats took full control of state government as the Minnesota Legislature convened Tuesday for its 2023 session, with leaders planning to use their new power to swiftly enact stronger protections for abortion rights.

title: Kentucky’s GOP-led legislature opens election-year session
shortDescription: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Republican lawmakers in Kentucky resumed their push Tuesday to continue cutting the state's individual income tax rate, as they convened for a 30-day session that will play out against the backdrop of the state's premiere political contest — the race for governor.

title: New documents detail Sen. Ron Johnson asking about electors
shortDescription: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The former chairman of the Wisconsin Republican Party said Republican Sen. Ron Johnson spoke to him weeks before Joe Biden assumed the presidency about having the state’s GOP-controlled Legislature, rather than voters, choose Wisconsin’s presidential electors, according to newly released documents from closed-door testimony to the House Jan.

title: House picks rural southern Ohio Republican as new speaker
shortDescription: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Ohio House lawmakers on Tuesday picked a Republican from rural southern Ohio as the GOP-led chamber’s next leader, after support shifted away from another representative who’d won an informal vote weeks ago for that powerful role in state politics.

title: New Hampshire Democrats to DNC: Please don't punish us in 2024 primary
shortDescription: New Hampshire Democrats say they have no ability to change state election laws to comply with the DNC's 2024 presidential nominating calendar mandate.

title: Kevin McCarthy fails to win votes for speaker
shortDescription: GOP leader Kevin McCarthy failed to win 218 votes to become speaker of the House after three rounds of voting.

title: Biden and McConnell appear together, striking contrast to Kevin McCarthy House chaos
shortDescription: Biden and McConnell will make a rare appearance together in Kentucky to tout $1.6 billion approved for a major bridge project across the Ohio River.

title: What happens after McCarthy loses speaker votes? The process explained
shortDescription: Candidates for speaker are nominated from the floor by members of their parties. The speaker who is chosen does not have to be a member of Congress.

title: GOP rebels reject McCarthy as House adjourns without a speaker: recap
shortDescription: GOP turmoil sank Republican Rep. Kevin McCarthy on three votes for speaker of the House in the new Congress. Dem. Rep. Hakeem Jeffries also got votes.

title: OnPolitics: House GOP fails to elect new leader in historic first
shortDescription: Not since 1923 has the vote for a new speaker needed more than one ballot. Here's what's next.

title: After George Santos is sworn into Congress, could he be impeached?
shortDescription: George Santos, a newly elected New York Republican congressman, admitted last week to lying to voters about his life. What can Congress do about it?

title: Notable lawmakers to know: Who will be leading Congress in 2023?
shortDescription: It's the first day of the 118th Congress which means a fresh set of senators and representatives are being seated. Here are the lawmakers to watch.

title: McCarthy calls out Gaetz over speaker election
shortDescription: Rep. Kevin McCarthy is facing challenges from other Republicans in his bid to be elected speaker of the House.

title: Who are the Republicans balking at Kevin McCarthy as a House speaker?
shortDescription: There are two main groups of GOP lawmakers who could stand in Kevin McCarthy's way of becoming the next House speaker when the new Congress is seated.

title: How a new Congress will change the direction of the country: What we know
shortDescription: House GOP Leader Kevin McCarthy said having Republicans in the majority will change the direction of the country. The new Congress is sworn in Jan. 3.

title: Culture wars: House Republicans attack Pentagon for 'woke' policies
shortDescription: House Republicans plan to open a new front in the culture wars: attacking the Pentagon for its "woke" social policies.

title: As GOP takes the House, a blockade for Biden's congressional agenda
shortDescription: President Joe Biden’s agenda will largely be blunted in a divided Congress, with GOP lawmakers pledging to block Democrats' legislative priorities.

title: Trump hosts New Year's Eve party, ending year with legal, political setbacks
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump had some legal and political notable setbacks in 2022. He ended it with his annual New Year's Eve party at Mar-a-Lago.

title: Week one of the new Congress: Picking a speaker, investigations, more
shortDescription: The 118th Congress is due to be seated on Tuesday and lawmakers have some housekeeping before they can get to business. Here's what to keep an eye on.

title: State AG: Ex-Trump chief of staff Meadows won’t face NC voter fraud charges
shortDescription: Mark Meadows, former White House chief of staff to Trump, won’t face voter fraud charges for his 2020 registration and absentee vote, N.C. AG said.

title: What happened this year in politics? Midterm elections, Roe overturned
shortDescription: In a busy year for politics, the midterm elections shared the stage with the Supreme Court, ex-President Trump's legal troubles and more during 2022.

title: OnPolitics special edition: Trump's tax returns released
shortDescription: Nearly 6,000 pages include personal returns, business returns.

title: Top takeaways from Donald Trump's tax returns
shortDescription: Six years of former President Donald Trump's tax returns have been released by Congress after an eight-year legal battle.

title: Ex-President Donald Trump's tax returns released: What we know
shortDescription: The release contained six years of tax returns over the time Donald Trump was president and campaigned for the White House, from 2015 to 2020.

title: Biden is granting six pardons. Here's who they are
shortDescription: The White House said President Joe Biden granted clemency through a deliberative process in coordination with the Department of Justice.

title: Read the documents: Donald Trump's tax returns released
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump's personal and business tax returns over six years include thousands of pages. Read a copy of Trump's tax returns here.

title: On tropical vacation, Biden mulls: To be or not to be (a 2024 candidate)?
shortDescription: President Joe Biden, on new year's holiday in St. Croix, says he plans to run for the White House but wants to talk a 2024 campaign over with family.

title: Biden signs $1.7 trillion bill funding government operations
shortDescription: President Joe Biden signed a $1.7 trillion spending bill to keep the federal government operating through federal budget year-end in September 2023.

title: Ex-Transportation secretary Elaine Chao addresses Trump's "racist taunt"
shortDescription: Chao and her husband, Republican Kentucky Sen. Mitch McConnell, typically stay mum about former President Donald Trump's inflammatory comments.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The IRS Hasn’t Released Nearly Half a Million Nonprofit Tax Records
shortDescription: If you’re trying to evaluate a charity this year, you might have a hard time. The IRS is behind on releasing Form 990s, limiting access to key financial information the public uses to evaluate the nation’s tax-exempt companies.

title: How Far Right Are the 20 Republicans Who Voted Against McCarthy
shortDescription: Most denied the 2020 election, are members of the ultraconservative Freedom Caucus, or both.By Danielle Ivory, Charlie Smart and Karen Yourish

title: John Fetterman Got a New Suit for His Senate Swearing-In
shortDescription: The Pennsylvania lawmaker joins the Washington establishment. Sort of.By Vanessa Friedman

title: Hakeem Jeffries Takes Over as Leader of House Democrats
shortDescription: A lawyer and disciplined tactician, Mr. Jeffries is the first Black politician to lead either party in Congress.By Nicholas Fandos

title: McCarthy is short on votes to become speaker. Here’s what could happen next.
shortDescription: There is little modern precedent to govern the chaos that could ensue from a deadlock.By Catie Edmondson

title: Here’s how to watch the House speaker vote.
shortDescription: Kevin McCarthy’s bid to lead the House hangs in the balance as lawmakers gather on Tuesday to elect a speaker.By Zach Montague

title: The Pelosi Era Comes to a Close
shortDescription: The first woman to become speaker leaves a legacy that will be difficult for her successors to equal.By Carl Hulse
